So, as a digital agency owner, there's a lot of cool stuff that you can do. There's a lot of incredible lifestyles that you could lead, depending on the lifestyle that you want to build, in my opinion, is dependent on the type of business that you want to run and how you can structure this whole thing. An advanced tip for you is to build your entire business around the lifestyle that you want. Let's be honest, if you don't want to be the guy who's running a team of 19 people like I do, who's constantly on the phone with clients, constantly taking action, constantly recording programs, constantly in action on stages, on speaking gigs, on radio shows, on podcasts, doing whatever, and being in front of somebody all the time, if you don't want to be like that, you don't have to be. My lifestyle is still pretty cool. I manage to actually do things along the way, like be in great locations wherever I'm shooting or doing business at, traveling to both coasts, specifically Los Angeles and Miami, being with incredible people from all over the world who are the greatest mentors and teachers of all time because those are our ideal clients. So, work's fun, lifestyle's fun, everything for me is really cool. That being said, I also do things because I have a girlfriend, so I do have some kind of lifestyle outside of just work as well that I like to incorporate and balance. I get about three to four workouts in a week, typically three with a personal trainer for about an hour, so I get an additional impact whenever I do go to the gym those three times a week. I'm trying to get it up to five, but it's a lifestyle change and I will have to get used to it. I've also changed my diet severely, so I'd recommend as far as lifestyle choices to also eat clean. So, you have a consistent energy throughout your day, and so ongoing, long term as well, you can sustain that energy through the later years of your life. All that being said, I encourage you when it comes to a lifestyle, build around the things that you want. Go buy nice clothes, go spend money on nice things that you'd like. Don't just work for a business that you work 80 hours a week for and not take home any money. One of the absolute saddest cases in entrepreneurial history that I've ever seen currently for somebody who is absolutely working their face off and just ruining their life at the same time, both long term and remotely short term, is Gary Vandercuk, and I'm going to explain why. This man has 890 people who work for him. He constantly puts out videos about how stressful everything is and how fucked up running a big business is and how much work it takes when in reality you don't have to have a kind of business like that. You see, people absolutely crush Gary Vandercuk's numbers in business and run businesses where they're often Turks and Caicos with their feet kicked up where Gary Vandercuk has to go around the entire world every single day and be in front of everybody at all times while running one of the most stressful businesses with 890 employees and five of the most expensive cities that you can possibly have a staff. His overhead is so much and this man only takes home $2-3 million a year. Why would you ever try to generate more than $100 million in business and only take home $2-3 million a year? In his case, it's for legacy. He has different intentions when you may have, when I may have. You see, I personally am not in it to work $100 million worth of work and run a company that large and literally work as much as Gary does and only take home $2-3 million. You see, I would do the exact same thing that Gary does, but I would do it much more stress free. I would meditate a lot more than what I imagine that man does and I would pocket a lot more than $2-3 million measly to work for $100 million. Gary, see the legacy of this, I understand and the legacy part of it is what enthuses him to keep doing that at that level. I'm sure he has something up his sleeve that if somebody may send him this video for him to watch or as somebody who's a friend of a friend, he ends up seeing this. I hope he understands that I get, he may be doing it for a long term position where his agency all becomes more lucrative. I really hope that he has some long term plan where he sells this thing from monster price point and that's where he makes all of his money on the exit. I hope that at least in the short term, he literally understands that it is actually working towards it and isn't just going to say that in order to validate or back up this ego statement that I kind of just provoked from him. You have to know what kind of business you want to run because your lifestyle is going to get fucked up if you just build a big business like Gary did and you didn't think of your lifestyle. You have to know how much time you want to spend with your family. You have to know how much money you want to spend on clothes, cars, material items, food, what kind of food you want to eat, where you want to live, how much you want to spend on rent and of course through time it will change. Each time that you upgrade in business, make sure that you maintain the upgrade. Don't just go in, this is the greatest piece of advice I can give you. This is time and this is money and through time as you go to make more money, you get these occasional times where you have those lump sums. Don't get married in this lump sum is a nice quote that my business consultant gives me. A.K.A. don't go buy a bunch of shit that you shouldn't hear because guess what? It's unrealistic for you to maintain the affluence and then you're going to go back down here to normal. You might occasionally get these hits and each time you spend a bunch of money here, you don't want to increase your expenses. You see if this purple line represents your expenses, your expenses typically fall below what you make but right here your expenses may increase. Even though your income decreased, your expenses may remain the same. You got a big lump sum here, you paid off whatever expenses were needed but you still ended up spending more. Each time people typically get some kind of lump sum, their expenses typically trend up but their income does not trend the same. Until you have the ability to retain power and once again power means that as you go in from a normal condition into affluence, you maintain at a higher rate. This is the only time after you've retained the power that you can go in and spend a little more than what you are. Besides that, my greatest lifestyle tip to you would be don't increase your overhead as you increase that business for a small period of time. Make sure that you go into power first, don't increase your expenses and more importantly, don't let your business run you. Use Gary VandenChuk is an example for a digital agency owner who's working their face off burying themselves into the ground from a health standard and from an ability to I'm sure live longer than my most over so you're going to be able to do. He's going to have a short little debt. That being said, don't work for $100 million, only take on two to three. Work for whatever it is that you want to work for and take on however much you want to work for it. Work with your business. Don't let your business work on you. Some advanced tips on lifestyle tips inside of phase number eight. I'll see you guys in the next video.
